notes: Linear qualification. Content set by DfE and common across all exam boards. Pure maths accounts for approximately 2/3 of content; Statistics and Mechanics together account for approximately 1/3. Students must study all topics.

papers.name: Paper 1
papers.description: Pure Mathematics only. Any pure content may be assessed.

papers.name: Paper 2
papers.description: Any pure content plus Mechanics content (Vectors, Quantities and units, Kinematics, Forces and Newton's laws, Moments).

papers.name: Paper 3
papers.description: Any pure content plus Statistics content (Statistical sampling, Data presentation, Probability, Statistical distributions, Statistical hypothesis testing).

subtopics.name: Understand and use proof by deduction
Understand and use proof by exhaustion
Understand and use disproof by counter-example
Understand and use proof by contradiction (including proof of irrationality of √2 and infinity of primes)
Indices - laws of indices for rational exponents
Surds - manipulate surds, rationalise denominators
Quadratic functions - solve, complete square, discriminant, nature of roots
Simultaneous equations - analytical solution of two simultaneous equations in two unknowns
Inequalities - linear and quadratic, solution using algebra and graphs
Polynomials - manipulate, factorise, sketch, division, factor theorem, remainder theorem
Graphs - sketch polynomials, reciprocals, exponential, transformations y=af(x), y=f(x)+a, y=f(x+a), y=f(ax)
Functions - modulus, composite, inverse including domain and range
Partial fractions - decomposition of rational functions into partial fractions (denominators not more complicated than repeated linear)
Algebraic division - factor and remainder theorems
Modulus function - solve equations and inequalities involving |f(x)|

topics.name: Coordinate Geometry in the (x, y) Plane

subtopics.name: Equation of a straight line in various forms
Gradient and intercept, parallel and perpendicular lines
Equation of a circle - centre any point, radius
Use of properties of circle: angle in a semicircle, perpendicular from centre to chord
Equation of tangent and normal at a given point
Parametric equations - convert between Cartesian and parametric, sketch curves
Binomial expansion for positive integer n
Binomial expansion for any rational n
Arithmetic sequences and series - nth term, sum of n terms
Geometric sequences and series - nth term, sum of n terms, sum to infinity
Sigma notation
Recurrence relations
Trigonometric ratios - sine, cosine, tangent and reciprocals
Trigonometric identities - tan θ = sin θ/cos θ, sin²θ + cos²θ = 1
Solve simple trigonometric equations in given intervals
Radian measure - use radians in calculus and formulae for arc length and sector area
Small angle approximations
Secant, cosecant, cotangent - definitions, identities, equations
Compound angle formulae - sin(A±B), cos(A±B), tan(A±B)
Double angle formulae
Harmonic form R cos(θ±α), R sin(θ±α)
Proving trigonometric identities
Inverse trigonometric functions
Exponential functions y = aˣ, including graph of y = eˣ
Logarithms - definition, laws of logarithms, change of base
Solve equations of form aˣ = b
Use logarithmic graphs to establish relationships in form y = axⁿ and y = kbˣ
Exponential growth and decay models including use of e
Natural logarithms - ln x
Differentiation from first principles
Differentiate xⁿ, including fractional and negative exponents
Tangents and normals
Stationary points - maxima, minima, points of inflection
Chain rule, product rule, quotient rule
Differentiate eˣ, ln x, sin x, cos x, tan x and related functions
Differentiate using parametric and implicit differentiation
Construct simple differential equations
Connected rates of change
Second derivatives and their application to curve sketching
Integrate xⁿ (including n = -1) and related functions
Indefinite integrals
Definite integrals - evaluate and interpret areas
Integrate eˣ, 1/x, sin x, cos x
Integration by substitution
Integration by parts
Trapezium rule
Solve first order separable differential equations
Locate roots by considering sign changes of f(x)
Iterative formulae in form xₙ₊₁ = f(xₙ)
Newton-Raphson method
Use numerical methods to solve problems in context
2D vectors - use vectors in component form and column vector notation
Magnitude and direction of a vector
Position vectors
Solve geometric problems using vectors
3D vectors and coordinates (x, y, z)
Vectors in mechanics problems
Populations, samples, sampling methods
Types of data - quantitative, qualitative, continuous, discrete
The Large Data Set - understand context, clean data, use spreadsheet
Measures of central tendency and spread - mean, median, mode, range, variance, standard deviation, IQR
Representations of data - box plots, cumulative frequency, histograms
Correlation and regression - scatter diagrams, PMCC, regression line y on x
Exponential models and PMCC
Hypothesis testing for correlation
Data cleaning and outlier detection
Venn diagrams and probability rules
Tree diagrams and independence
Conditional probability
Set notation and probability formulae
Probability distributions - discrete random variables
The Binomial distribution - conditions, notation, probabilities, mean and variance
The Normal distribution - notation N(μ,σ²), standard normal, use tables/calculator
Selecting an appropriate distribution
Approximating Binomial with Normal
Understand terms null hypothesis, alternative hypothesis, significance level, test statistic, critical region, critical value
Conduct statistical hypothesis test using Binomial distribution
Understand errors - Type I and Type II
Conduct hypothesis test with Normal distribution
Hypothesis testing for correlation using PMCC
SI units and base quantities - mass (kg), length (m), time (s)
Derived quantities - force (N), velocity, acceleration
Scalars and vectors - distinguish between scalar and vector quantities
Displacement-time and velocity-time graphs
SUVAT equations for constant acceleration
Vertical motion under gravity
Projectiles - horizontal and inclined projection
Variable acceleration using calculus
Force diagrams and forces as vectors
F = ma, weight = mg
Equilibrium of forces - resultant force, resolving forces
Dynamics - connected particles, pulleys
Friction - coefficient of friction, limiting equilibrium
Newton's three laws of motion
Modelling assumptions - particle, smooth, rough, light, inextensible
Understand and use moment of a force
Equilibrium of rigid bodies - sum of moments, sum of forces
Simple frameworks and ladders problems
